narrative:
Roberto Alvarado is Mexico's right winger, currently holding an overall rating of 7.14/10. This impressive score places him in the top 4% of right wingers at this tournament. His rating range of 6.81–7.48 reflects a consistent impact, with 35% of his overall rating attributed to his World Cup performances across 430 minutes played.

Alvarado demonstrates significant creative and defensive contributions, highlighted by his 0.628 assists per 90 minutes and 2.721 key passes per 90 minutes at this World Cup. He also contributes defensively with 1.884 tackles and 1.884 clearances per 90 minutes. However, his direct goal threat is limited, with only 0.045 expected goals and 0.628 shots per 90 minutes.

Alvarado plays a vital role for Mexico as a creative wide player who also offers defensive support. Scouts and fans should continue to watch his ability to provide key passes and assists in the remaining matches. It will also be interesting to see if he can increase his direct attacking output and goal-scoring threat.
